steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage claims opposite-side orientation was ‘much more common’ than same-side in the Amur grape. The blank must complete that claim using specific numbers from the graph. The correct choice needs the right values for both opposite-side and same-side counts.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '140 opposite-side, ~60 same-side': matches the graph's dark gray bars for Amur grape — roughly 140 opposite-side and 60 same-side pairs. ✓
  • Choice B — '140 opposite-side, ~45 same-side': the same-side value is wrong; the graph shows approximately 60, not 45. ✗
  • Choice C — '140 opposite-side, 0 same-side': misstates same-side as zero; the graph shows a non-zero bar. ✗
  • Choice D — '140 opposite-side, 0 same-side': also misstates same-side as zero. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
